question.text: What is the order matching principle used in the ETIRD trading system?

answer.text: Price-time priority: best price is matched first; among same-price orders, earliest order is matched first
Random allocation among all orders at the same price
Pro-rata matching based on order size

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The ETIRD trading system uses price-time priority: the best-priced order (highest bid or lowest ask) gets matched first. If multiple orders are at the same price, the one entered earliest gets priority. **The Trap:** Random allocation is used in some auction systems. Pro-rata is used in some specific futures exchanges. FCFS ignoring price is not used in any exchange. **Cross-Exam Context:** Price-time priority is the standard matching algorithm across all Indian exchange-traded segments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This means if you want your order filled faster, either offer a better price or place your order earlier.
